text.title: perplexity-ai-research Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for perplexity-ai-research. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Use search_recency_filter: "year" in API calls to avoid stale data; Perplexity defaults to broader time windows that include outdated figures.
rule.rationale: Market data from 2022 presented as current can be off by a factor of 2-3x. Recency filtering is not the default; it must be set explicitly.

rule.statement: Formulate one atomic fact per query; compound queries ("market size AND competitors AND trends") produce diluted answers across all three sub-questions.
rule.rationale: Pro Search distributes its retrieval budget across all parts of a compound query. Atomic queries get the full retrieval budget for one fact.

rule.statement: Citations are URL-level, not page-level; a cited source may support only part of the synthesized claim.
rule.rationale: Perplexity's citation granularity is coarse; claiming full source support requires manual spot-check.

rule.statement: Perplexity's synthesis can merge facts from different time periods without flagging the temporal mismatch.
rule.rationale: Temporal misalignment can lead to incorrect conclusions; agent must enforce explicit year statements.

rule.statement: Paywalled sources appear in citation lists but Perplexity cannot read behind the paywall — summary reflects only the abstract or preview.
rule.rationale: Paywalled sources in a citation list create false credibility; the agent cannot verify full-article support.

rule.statement: For rapidly changing data (live stats, breaking news), Perplexity's index lags real-time by hours to days.
rule.rationale: Real-time precision is impossible; Perplexity is suitable for strategic research, not live monitoring.

rule.statement: Only searches the public web; cannot access PDFs, internal databases, or proprietary data.
rule.rationale: Scope is limited to publicly accessible content.

rule.statement: Cross-validate high-stakes numbers (TAM, funding rounds, revenue) against at least one additional independent source (AlphaSense, Crunchbase, official press releases).
rule.rationale: Perplexity's synthesis sounds authoritative even when it cites a single low-quality source. Citation count and source quality must be checked, not just the answer text.

rule.statement: Decompose a research question into 3-5 atomic sub-queries before executing any Pro Search; each sub-query retrieves one fact with one expected source type.
rule.rationale: Single-query compound research dilutes retrieval across all sub-questions. Atomic decomposition maximizes depth on each individual fact and makes citation validation tractable.

rule.statement: Always export citations and store them alongside the synthesized finding; the source is as important as the fact for downstream validation.
rule.rationale: Without stored citations, findings cannot be audited, attributed, or updated when the source changes.

rule.statement: Assign confidence levels per synthesized claim: H = multiple independent sources agree; M = single credible source or partial agreement; L = single source or paywalled.
rule.rationale: Confidence ratings force honest calibration and surface which claims need additional validation before external presentation.

rule.statement: Human review required for any Perplexity-sourced figure that will be presented externally; hallucination risk is lower than pure LLM but not zero.
rule.rationale: Perplexity synthesizes from real sources but can misattribute, merge temporally distinct facts, or cite a source that supports only part of the synthesized claim.

rule.statement: Flag paywalled citations as unverified; the agent cannot confirm whether the full article supports the claim.
rule.rationale: A paywalled citation in the result list gives the appearance of source support without actual access to the source content.

rule.statement: Add exponential backoff and retry logic to any batch Perplexity API pipeline; rate limits on the Pro API are strict at lower tiers.
rule.rationale: Batch research pipelines without retry logic fail silently on rate-limit errors and return incomplete results that appear complete.

rule.statement: Perplexity's synthesis sounds authoritative even when it cites a single low-quality source; always check citation count and source quality, not just the answer text.
rule.rationale: Confidence in answer text is not confidence in source quality.

rule.statement: The API returns citations as URLs, not structured metadata; agents must parse and validate each URL independently if source quality assessment is required.
rule.rationale: Automated source quality checking requires programmatic inspection; URL-only citations are insufficient.

rule.statement: Use Perplexity Spaces to organize a research project's queries and findings across sessions; prevents duplication and enables audit trails.
rule.rationale: Spaces provide project organization and session continuity.

rule.statement: Cross-validate high-stakes numbers (TAM, funding rounds, revenue) against at least one additional independent source (AlphaSense, Crunchbase, official press releases).
rule.rationale: Second sourcing increases confidence in mission-critical data.

rule.statement: Formulate one atomic fact per query; compound queries dilute answer quality across all sub-questions.
rule.rationale: Atomic queries maximize retrieval depth per fact.

rule.statement: Always export citations and store them alongside the synthesized finding — the source is as important as the fact for downstream validation.
rule.rationale: Source preservation enables auditing and updates.
